disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Onset was triggered by minor physical exertion the day before; symptoms have been present ~24 hours
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when this started?
Did anything bring it on — exertion, activity?
When did this begin and what set it off?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started yesterday after I pushed myself a bit — did more around the house than I should have. It's been there ever since.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chest discomfort is a diffuse central tight band, NOT radiating to the jaw or arm, only minimally worse on deep inspiration
trigger_question_examples: Does the chest discomfort spread anywhere — into your jaw, neck, or arm?
Is the pain in one spot or spread across, and does it move?
Does it change when you breathe in deeply?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it doesn't shoot into my arm or jaw — it's more of a tight band across the whole front of my chest. It nips a little more if I take a really deep breath, but that's all.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: It's just a tight feeling across my chest, I haven't really thought about where it goes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Air hunger / sensation of not being able to take a satisfying deep breath, with a persistent dry cough
trigger_question_examples: Tell me about your breathing — what does it feel like?
Any cough or other chest symptoms?
Are you short of breath, and what makes it worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's like I can't fill my lungs properly — air hunger, I've heard it called. And I've got this annoying dry cough that keeps going.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: This is a 24-hour exacerbation of known long COVID / post-viral symptoms; patient has had similar chest and breathing flares before but rates this one as worse
trigger_question_examples: Have you had anything like this before?
Is this the same as your usual long COVID flares or different?
How does this compare to your normal symptoms?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I do get chest tightness and breathing flare-ups with my long COVID, so it's not brand new — but this one feels stronger and it's lasted longer than usual.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe fatigue and high anxiety, specifically frightened it is heart failure; lives alone, which compounds the fear and the breathlessness sensation
trigger_question_examples: This sounds really frightening — how are you coping on your own with it?
What's worrying you most about these symptoms?
You mentioned you're scared — can you tell me what you're afraid it might be?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I'm terrified it's my heart failing. I'm on my own here and every time I feel breathless I panic, which I think makes the breathing worse.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Red-flag screen NEGATIVE on patient-obtainable data: no coughing up blood, pain is not crushing and does not radiate to jaw/arm, no severe breathlessness at rest (rest eases it), no new calf swelling or pain that the patient has noticed
trigger_question_examples: Have you coughed up any blood?
Is the pain crushing, or does it spread to your jaw or arm?
Are you gasping for air even when you sit completely still, or is there swelling or pain in your calf?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blood when I cough. It's not a crushing pain and it doesn't go into my arm or jaw. When I sit still it does ease off a bit, and I haven't noticed any swelling or pain in my legs.
